stem: A programmer uses a bubble sort to put a list of integers into ascending order.
model_answer: A bubble sort works by comparing adjacent pairs of items and swapping them if they are in the wrong order. After a full pass through the list, the largest remaining item has "bubbled" to the end. The algorithm then repeats from the start, ignoring the sorted items at the end, until a pass is made with no swaps (the list is sorted). Bubble sort is easy to implement but slow on large lists because it may do many passes.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how a bubble sort sorts a list into ascending order.

mark_points.text: compare each adjacent pair of items in the list
accepted_alternatives: look at two items next to each other

mark_points.text: swap them if they are in the wrong order (larger before smaller)
repeat passes through the list until a pass completes with no swaps (list is sorted)
accepted_alternatives: keep repeating passes until no more swaps are needed
